greeting: ^Ah, Barrington. I’m glad you made it out of the forest! It’s feeling dark down here, I know. You look weary, my friend.
^I hope I can be of some help. I came across a hunk of wood here. I hear you’re building yourself a ship! Feel free to use it. It would be good for you to let yourself out of this place.

^As a scientist, I have to remember that there’s more than one answer to a problem. If something isn’t working, I move onto something else. If you’ve outgrown this place here, why not try something new yourself? There’s always an alternative!
^Why not start now? When you leave this place, what’s something else you might try?
farewell: ^Amazing! The best scientists always have different levels of study. I wish you the best of luck.
